name: Lovelight Chocolate Chiffon Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 Eggs; separated (1 for whites, 1 for yolks)
1 1/2 cups Sugar; divided into two amounts
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Grease and flour two 9-inch cake pans.
For the cake batter:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg whites until frothy (about 2 minutes).
2. Gradually add half of the sugar and continue beating until whites hold stiff peaks (about 5 minutes). Set aside.
3. In another large mixing bowl, sift together sifted cake flour, remaining cup of sugar, baking soda, and salt.
4. Add oil and half of the buttermilk; beat for one minute, constantly scraping down the sides and bottom of the bowl.
5. Add remaining half of buttermilk, egg yolks, and melted chocolate. Beat until well combined.
6. Gently fold the reserved meringue into the flour-buttermilk mixture until combined (do not overmix).
7. Pour batter into prepared pans.
8. Bake at 350 degrees until a wooden toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean (about 25 to 30 minutes).
9. Remove pans to wire cooling racks and let cake cool in pans for 10 minutes.
10. Remove cake from pans and cool completely on racks.
For the frosting:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, beat egg whites until stiff enough to hold a point when the beaters are lifted (about 4 minutes).
2. Combine sugar, water, and corn syrup in a medium-sized, heavy-bottomed saucepan.
3. Cover and bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
4. Uncover, and cook until the mixture measures 242 degrees on a candy thermometer.
5. In a thin stream, slowly pour hot sugar syrup into the beaten egg whites. Continue beating until stiff peaks form when the beaters are lifted.
6. Stir in vanilla.
7. Frost cake and serve.
Source: A 1956 Betty Crocker recipe printed in the Chicago Sun Times, November 6, 1996
